items.title: Targeting tumor-associated nerves enhances cancer immunotherapy
items.author: Canhui Cao
items.content: <article>Tumor-associated nerves actively shape tumor immunity. In a recent issue of Nature, Baruch et al. show that cancer-induced nerve injury drives T cell exhaustion and undermines PD-1 blockade. Targeting this neuro-immune crosstalk is a promising strategy to enhance cancer immunotherapy.</article>

items.title: Decoding acupuncture somatotopic maps for neuromodulation
items.author: Luis Ulloa, Yong-Qing Yang
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Dong et al.1 describe neural pathways involved in electroacupuncture (EA) stimulation of gastric motility in mice. Their findings improve treatments for functional dyspepsia patients. Recent breakthroughs in the field enable emerging bioelectronic neuromodulation therapies for gastrointestinal disorders.</article>

items.title: Microglial ADGRG1: AD glial resilience generator
items.author: Kia M. Barclay, Qingyun Li
items.content: <article>Microglial states underlying Alzheimer’s disease (AD) have been well characterized in animal models and human samples, yet their regulation remains elusive. In this issue of Neuron, Zhu et al. uncover Adgrg1, which governs a protective microglia phenotype through MYC activation.</article>

items.title: From genetic predisposition to mental health: Human cortical development in vitro
items.author: Arghya Mukherjee, Pico Caroni
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Walsh et al. show how an improved human forebrain assembloid system that includes bona fide developing and differentiated cortical parvalbumin interneurons can be harnessed to investigate early human cortical development in genetic models of schizophrenia.</article>

items.title: Unraveling neurovascular disease risk with MultiVINE-seq
items.author: Lynn van Olst, David Gate
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Reid et al. introduce MultiVINE-seq, a single-nucleus multi-omic platform for profiling human cerebrovascular and perivascular cells for paired transcriptomic and epigenomic profiling. By integrating genome-wide association data, they map neurodegenerative and cerebrovascular disease risk variants to specific vascular and immune cell types, uncovering distinct and convergent pathogenic pathways.</article>

items.title: Dysfunction of cortical GABAergic projection neurons as a major hallmark in a model of neuropsychiatric syndrome
items.author: Andrea Asenjo-Martinez, Katarina Dragicevic, Wen-Hsien Hou, Attila Ozsvar, Nikolaj Winther Hansen, Ulrich Pfisterer, Rasmus Rydbirk, Samuel Demharter, Bente Emma Møller, Janina Gasthaus, Irina Korshunova, Jean-François Perrier, Marco Capogna, Navneet A. Vasistha, Konstantin Khodosevich
items.content: <article>Using advanced transcriptomic and physiological profiling, the authors identified deep-layer long-range GABAergic projection neurons as the most disrupted neuronal subtype and key contributor to brain dysfunction in the 15q13.3 neuropsychiatric mouse model. Late-onset impairments in these neurons disrupted sleep, which was rescued by chemogenetic inhibition, offering mechanistic insight and therapeutic potential.</article>

items.title: Gut-induced alpha-Synuclein and Tau propagation initiate Parkinson’s and Alzheimer’s disease co-pathology and behavior impairments
items.author: Jie Xiang, Jingrong Tang, Fei Kang, Jiajun Ye, Yueying Cui, Zhentao Zhang, Jing Wang, Shengxi Wu, Keqiang Ye

items.title: On astrocyte-neuron interactions: Broad insights from the striatum
items.author: Baljit S. Khakh
items.content: <article>In this review, Khakh discusses the mechanisms, physiology, and pathophysiological relevance of astrocyte-neuron interactions across biological scales from molecules to whole organisms. By highlighting findings from the striatum, Khakh identifies insights of significance to other brain areas and summarizes key open questions and challenges.</article>

items.title: Alterations in a cross-hemispheric circuit associates with novelty discrimination deficits in mouse models of neurodegeneration
items.author: Chinnakkaruppan Adaikkan, Jun Wang, Karim Abdelaal, Steven J. Middleton, P. Lorenzo Bozzelli, Ian R. Wickersham, Thomas J. McHugh, Li-Huei Tsai

items.title: N-Glycosylation Is Essential for Vesicular Targeting of Synaptotagmin 1
items.author: Weiping Han, Jeong-Seop Rhee, Anton Maximov, Ye Lao, Tomoyuki Mashimo, Christian Rosenmund, Thomas C. Südhof
